pain.label: The Problem
pain.title: The Three Hidden Costs Killing Tutoring Margins
pain.subtitle: Not rent. Not marketing. It's the slow, manual work of content creation, one-size-fits-all review, and the complete absence of learning analytics.

items.title: Content Creation Drains Your Best People
items.desc: A single quality question takes 30 minutes — topic selection, stem writing, plausible distractors, detailed explanations. A full mock exam consumes an entire week of your best instructor's time.

items.title: Every Student Gets the Same Review Plan
items.desc: Your top students re-drill what they already know. Your struggling students are thrown into material they're not ready for. Nobody learns at their actual frontier.

items.title: You're Flying Blind on Student Progress
items.desc: You discover knowledge gaps at the end-of-month Excel review — weeks after the student needed intervention. By then, the exam is already over.

features.label: Core Capabilities
features.title: Three engines. One platform.
features.subtitle: AI generates the content. Memorix schedules the review. The Knowledge Workbench maps mastery. A complete pipeline from content production to learning outcomes.

items.title: AI Question Generation Engine
items.subtitle: Adversarial pipeline. Production-ready output.
items.desc: Three specialized AI agents — Author, Reviewer, and Classifier — collaborate adversarially on every question. The Author drafts. The Reviewer critiques for accuracy, clarity, and distractor quality. The Classifier validates subject tags and difficulty. Substandard generations are rejected and retried — up to 3 iterations — until they cross a calibrated quality threshold. Compared to a single LLM call, our adversarial pipeline lifts the usable-question rate from ~60% to 85%+. Multiple choice, calculation, case analysis, essay — all question types, across any subject. Finance, medicine, law, accounting — the engine adapts to each discipline's academic conventions.
points: Author → Reviewer → Classifier: adversarial pipeline, up to 3 iterations
Auto quality gating with configurable thresholds, 85%+ usability
All question types, all subjects — discipline-aware generation

items.title: Memorix Adaptive Review Engine
items.subtitle: Research-grade forgetting models. Review right before you forget.
items.desc: Memorix is our proprietary memory-scheduling algorithm — fundamentally different from traditional spaced repetition. It models forgetting with a Weibull distribution rather than a power law, capturing the 'steep-then-gradual' decay pattern of human memory with significantly higher precision. After every answer, the algorithm updates a 20-dimensional personal parameter vector via online stochastic gradient descent with Nesterov momentum, calibrated with Brier scores for probabilistic confidence. On our Finance 431 benchmark dataset (500+ users, 120,000 review logs), Memorix reduced prediction RMSE by 13.7% versus FSRS v4.5 and improved long-term knowledge retention by 9.2%.
points: Weibull forgetting curves — more accurate than exponential or power-law models
Online SGD + Nesterov momentum — every response fine-tunes your memory portrait
Brier Score calibration — forgetting predictions with mathematical guarantees
20-dimensional personalization — individual memory profiles, not population averages

items.title: Knowledge Workbench
items.subtitle: Interactive knowledge graph. Mastery at a glance.
items.desc: Every knowledge point appears as a color-coded node in an interactive SVG graph — green for mastered, red for weak, gray for untouched. Click any node to drill into associated questions, mistake history, and course resources. Students see exactly where they stand. Teachers pinpoint class-wide weak spots in seconds. Curriculum designers identify which topics produce the most errors.
points: Interactive SVG knowledge graph with zoom and pan
Mastery-based color coding — weak spots surface automatically
One-click navigation to questions, mistakes, and course materials

how.label: How It Works
how.title: Three steps. Ten minutes to launch.
how.subtitle: No engineering team. No AI expertise. No infrastructure to manage. Register → Generate questions → Students practice. That's the whole workflow.

steps.title: Pick Your Subject
steps.desc: Register your institution and select from pre-built knowledge frameworks spanning finance, medicine, law, accounting, STEM, and more. Or upload your own curriculum — the engine adapts immediately.

steps.title: AI Generates the Content
steps.desc: Open the AI Question Center, select knowledge points and question types. The three-agent adversarial pipeline generates, reviews, and quality-checks each question. Your subject-matter lead gives final approval — one click per batch.

steps.title: Students Practice. You Get Analytics.
steps.desc: Share a link or QR code — students jump in instantly on any device. Auto-grading after every answer. The Knowledge Workbench updates each student's mastery map in real time. No app installs, no onboarding friction.

subjects.label: All Subjects
subjects.title: Not tied to any one exam or curriculum.
subjects.subtitle: UniMind's AI engine is an expert in 'how to write a good question' — feed it any subject's knowledge points, and it generates in that discipline's voice. The platform doesn't care whether it's finance, medicine, or civil engineering.
subjects.footer: Don't see your subject? Custom knowledge trees are built in minutes. Tell us what you teach — we'll help you set it up.

pricing.label: Pricing
pricing.title: Plans for every stage
pricing.subtitle: All plans include a 14-day full-feature trial. No credit card required. Start free, upgrade when you're ready.
pricing.footer: First 20 paying customers lock in early-adopter pricing for life. Pricing in CNY — international billing coming soon.

items.q: Is UniMind only for Chinese exams?
items.a: No. The platform is fully subject-agnostic. Our AI engine generates questions in English, Chinese, or any language you prompt it with. We have pre-built knowledge frameworks for globally recognized exams — CFA, USMLE, CPA, Bar Exam, SAT, GRE — and you can upload your own curriculum in minutes. The three-agent adversarial pipeline works the same way regardless of subject or language.

items.q: Can the AI generate questions in English? What about other languages?
items.a: Yes. The AI engine is multilingual — it generates and reviews content in English by default for non-Chinese subjects, and you can specify any output language. Our international users (CFA, USMLE) receive English-language questions, explanations, and distractors. The platform UI currently supports Chinese and English, with more languages on the roadmap.

items.q: What makes Memorix different from Anki or spaced repetition apps?
items.a: Anki and similar tools use fixed-interval algorithms (SM-2, FSRS). Memorix uses a Weibull-distribution forgetting model that better captures real human memory decay, plus online stochastic gradient descent to personalize 20 parameters per learner after every response. In head-to-head testing on 120,000 review logs, Memorix reduced prediction error by 13.7% vs FSRS v4.5. It's not just 'when to review' — it's a learning-rate-adaptive scheduler.

items.q: How do my students access the platform?
items.a: Students access everything through a web link or QR code — no app to download, no software to install. The platform is mobile-optimized and works in any modern browser. You share a link, they start practicing. Zero friction.

items.q: What happens when the free trial ends?
items.a: All your data — questions, student records, learning analytics — remains intact. Renew your subscription to restore full access. Without renewal, your data is read-only. You never lose anything you've built.

items.q: Can I pay monthly? Do you support international payment methods?
items.a: Monthly and annual billing are both supported. Annual plans include a 23-33% discount. Currently, billing is in CNY via Chinese payment processors. International billing (USD, Stripe) is on our near-term roadmap. For now, international customers can pay via wire transfer or work through our partnerships team.

items.q: How is UniMind different from generic quiz platforms?
items.a: Quiz platforms digitize paper tests. UniMind produces and optimizes teaching content with AI. The difference: questions are auto-generated, not manually entered. Review is algorithm-personalized per student, not one-size-fits-all. Analytics are real-time and drillable to the knowledge-point level, not end-of-month PDFs. We solve 'how to teach and how to learn' — not just 'how to test.'

items.q: I run a small tutoring center. Which plan should I start with?
items.a: Start with Free. 30 students and 20 AI generations per month let you validate the full workflow — generate → student practice → analytics — with a small group. When you're ready to scale, Solo (¥299/mo, ¥199/mo annual) removes the limits. Most centers upgrade within the first month once they see the time savings.

items.q: Can I use UniMind alongside my existing LMS or website?
items.a: Pro plan includes REST API access for integration. You can embed UniMind's question engine and review system into your existing platform. For custom integrations beyond the API, our solutions team can scope a tailored deployment — contact us through the demo request.
